method: cross-site Pearson correlation of per-sign mean within-document-z-scored log glyph-bbox-area size profiles; sign-label-shuffle permutation null (>=2000 draws, one-sided); synthetic positive control (detect-shared + false-positive-idiosyncratic)
result: Normalized glyph size is a SHARED cross-site graphic convention: 5/5 testable site-pairs show significant positive size-profile correlation (r in [0.70,0.85], perm_p<=0.0016), far above the sign-label-shuffle null. Positive control passed (detect power=1.0, false-positive rate=0.0).
key_findings: 5 of 5 testable site-pairs (>=8 common signs with >=5 obs each) show significant positive cross-site correlation of per-sign mean normalized glyph size (perm_p<=0.0016, sign-label-shuffle null, 5000 draws).

Observed correlations are large and tightly positive: r=0.79 (Haghia Triada-Khania, n=25), r=0.82 (HT-Phaistos, n=11), r=0.70 (HT-Zakros, n=34), r=0.74 (Khania-Zakros, n=18), r=0.85 (Phaistos-Zakros, n=9); median r=0.79.
Anchor pairs (Haghia Triada vs each other big site) are all significant: HT-Khania, HT-Phaistos, HT-Zakros (HT-Knossos untestable, only 7 common signs).
Pattern matches the SHARED sign-frequency fingerprint (E036), NOT the site-local allograph-shape pattern (E017/E020): glyph size is a shared cross-site graphic convention.

Positive control calibrated: synthetic shared-convention data detected with power=1.0 (mean r=0.91); synthetic site-idiosyncratic data gave false-positive rate=0.0 (mean r=0.007), never reaching the >=2-significant-pairs gate.
successor_hypotheses: E077: Is the shared size convention driven by a small set of high-frequency signs, or is it uniform across the sign inventory? (per-sign contribution / leave-one-out).
E078: Does normalized glyph size correlate with a sign GRAPHIC COMPLEXITY proxy (e.g. stroke count / component count) cross-site — i.e. is the convention complexity-graded?
E079: Is the within-document size RANK of signs shared cross-site (ordinal convention) in addition to the z-scored magnitude?
E080: Do the underpowered sites (Knossos, Phaistos) join the shared convention once more docs are available (replicate with relaxed min-obs / pooled periods)?
E081: Is glyph size shared across PERIODS within a site, or is the convention period-locked (LM I vs LM III)?
E082: Cross-modal: does the shared size convention align with the shared sign-frequency fingerprint (E036) — are frequent signs drawn at a characteristic relative size?
non_circular: Within-document z-scoring of log bbox-area removes per-photo/per-tablet absolute pixel scale (no scale leak). The metric is the cross-site correlation of per-sign MEAN normalized size; the sign-label-shuffle null destroys the sign->size association, so any correlation is beyond chance sign-size pairing. Sign identity is an OPAQUE catalog ID (e.g. AB59) — no phonetic value, reading, or meaning is used or implied. L2 structural graphic property only.
deviations: Initial PC runs showed an inflated false-positive rate (up to 1.0) due to (a) a stale bytecode cache and (b) seed-coupling between data-generation and permutation RNG when both derived from the same base offset; resolved by clearing __pycache__ and fully decoupling the two RNG streams (data seed 1000+rep*7 / 2000+rep*7; perm RNG 9000+rep*13 / 8000+rep*13). Canonical PC with decoupled seeds: power=1.0, false_pos_rate=0.0. No change to the frozen metric, null, or verdict rule.
Glyphs with sign=None or non-string sign labels (a handful) were excluded from profile construction; this does not affect any testable pair (>=8 common signs threshold met regardless).
Knossos has only 7 signs with >=5 observations, so all Knossos pairs are untestable (<8 common signs); 5 of 10 site-pairs remain testable, all significant.
